title: Zahle, Ziit und Datum
desc: Numbers and time the Swiss way

sn.s: Bi de Ziit muess mer da chli ufpasse.
sn.e: With time you have to pay attention here.

sn.s: "Halbi achti" heisst halb acht, also sibni dryssg.
sn.e: "Halbi achti" means half past seven, i.e. seven thirty.

sn.s: Bim Zähle tönt's au chli anders.
sn.e: When counting it sounds a bit different too.

sn.s: "Füüf, sächs, sibe, acht": das chunnt schnäll.
sn.e: "Five, six, seven, eight": that comes quickly.

sn.s: Wenn mer en Termin abmacht, isch mer gnau.
sn.e: When you arrange an appointment, you're precise.

sn.s: "Am Viertel ab zwöi" heisst gnau das, nöd öppe spöter.
sn.e: "At quarter past two" means exactly that, not a bit later.

sn.s: Z'spät cho isch unhöflich, das han ich scho glärnt.
sn.e: Being late is rude, I've learned that already.

sn.s: Am Aafang han ich "halbi" falsch verstande.
Ich bi e halbi Stund z'früeh cho. Peinlich, aber luschtig.
sn.e: I arrived half an hour early. Embarrassing, but funny.

sn.s: Jetz verstah ich d'Ziit wie e Lozärner.
sn.e: Now I understand time like a local.

sn.s: Und ich bi immer pünktlich, versteht sich.
sn.e: And I'm always on time, naturally.
